spread-monitor.dynamic: Today's data reveals a consistent risk-off positioning across multiple market levels. The SPY/TLT ratio at 7.67 is below its 50-day average and falling (-0.47% slope), confirming a flight-to-safety trade into longer-duration bonds. Speculative sentiment has cooled notably, with ARKK/QQQ at 0.118—down 3.95% over five days and 3.38% over twenty days—suggesting meaningful de-risking out of growth and innovation plays. Meanwhile, small-cap confidence (IWM/SPY at 0.371) and emerging market appetite (EEM/SPY at 0.086) both remain depressed and below their moving averages, indicating investors are concentrating capital in large-cap US equities rather than taking broader risk. Credit conditions remain stable (HYG/LQD holding at 0.733), so the pullback appears driven by duration and growth concerns rather than systemic credit stress.

sector-rotation.dynamic:
# Market Analysis: Sector Rotation Dashboard

Today's data reveals a broad weakness across international and diversified equity ETFs, with most instruments showing negative returns across multiple timeframes. The Africa-focused AFK ETF stands out as the worst performer, down 6.80% over three days and 16.56% over ten days, despite maintaining a bullish historical pattern agreement score. Most bullish-rated ETFs (sr_score 3.5-4.2) like AAXJ, ACWI, and AIRR have experienced consistent losses across 3-day, 5-day, and 10-day windows, suggesting their bullish signals may be lagging current market deterioration. The lone exception is AGG (Aggregate Bond), which shows a near-flat score of 0.1 with minimal losses, indicating bonds have held up better than equities during this downturn. Notably, all prediction columns (pred_3d through pred_10d) show NaN values, meaning the historical pattern matching model has insufficient confidence to generate forward return predictions given current market conditions.

momentum-ranker.dynamic: Today's data shows TNGX and LINC as the top two momentum performers, with TNGX commanding rank #1 with a composite score of 97.6. TNGX displays extraordinary returns across all timeframes, most dramatically with 784.77% over one year and 154.82% over six months, while maintaining perfect positioning above all major moving averages (SMA 30/50/100/200). The consistency of TNGX's momentum is reinforced by strong ratio passes (4/5) and exceptional resilience on down SPY days with a score of 99.76, indicating it held gains even when the broader market declined. LINC follows as the second-ranked candidate with more modest but still substantial returns (145.8% one-year, 87.9% six-month) and similarly strong SMA alignment, though its one-year outperformance versus SPY (126.96%) significantly lags TNGX's 765.93%, suggesting TNGX's momentum is both longer-lasting and more independent of market direction. Both tickers show low correlation to defensive sectors (negative correlations to XLP, XLB, XLI), indicating their strength derives from sector-specific or fundamental drivers rather than broad market beta.

momentum-ranker-long.dynamic:
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker-Long

Today's data shows two exceptionally strong long-term momentum performers at the top of the ranking. MPC (Marathon Petroleum) claims the #1 rank with a score of 93.0, driven by robust 10-year returns of 517% and outperformance versus SPY by 286 percentage points over the same period; it passes all technical moving average filters and displays a "strong_buy" signal with clean momentum. FUWAY demonstrates even more extreme returns—1,075% over 10 years and 844% outperformance versus SPY—but its momentum profile shows a concerning 6.36x jump from 1-month to 3-month returns, suggesting a recent spike that warrants caution despite the massive long-term gains. Both tickers maintain all four major moving average supports (SMA30/50/100/200), though MPC's correlation analysis reveals heavy exposure to energy (XLE at 0.677), while FUWAY's negative SPY correlation score (-0.34) indicates it has diverged from broader market trends. The data captures a moment where energy sector momentum (MPC) and a high-volatility outlier (FUWAY) dominate the long-term rankings, suggesting investors seeking these profiles should understand the sector concentration and volatility risks involved.

similar-days.dynamic: Today's momentum profile shows an exceptionally strong match to the immediately preceding days, with 2026-03-12 correlating at 0.9452 and the prior two days also above 0.87, indicating the market is sustaining a very consistent directional pattern rather than shifting abruptly. Beyond the last three days, there is a sharp drop-off in correlation scores, suggesting today's momentum is distinctly different from typical historical conditions when examined across the full 25-year dataset. The secondary cluster of similar days pulls from volatile periods in 2002-2005 (tech recovery era), early 2022 (rate-hike period), and late 2023 (post-summer rally), all with correlations in the 0.61-0.67 range, implying today's setup shares some characteristics with past inflationary or policy-driven market rotations. The absence of any single strong historical analog beyond the recent three-day window suggests current market conditions are either building on recent momentum in an unusual way or represent a transitional phase not heavily repeated in prior decades.

historical-mirror.dynamic: Today's market fingerprint shows an exceptionally rare alignment, with a composite similarity score of 1.0000 matching only itself (March 9, 2026), indicating today's momentum and spread conditions are historically unique across the 25-year dataset. The next closest analog is September 10, 2001 with a 0.7804 composite score, driven entirely by perfect spread alignment (1.0000) but much weaker momentum match (0.5609), suggesting spreads today resemble that post-9/11 period while momentum dynamics differ significantly. Most historical analogs cluster in the 0.58-0.66 range and span multiple market regimes (2000 tech crash, 2001 recession, 2011 debt crisis, 2024 recent period), indicating today's combination of momentum and spreads lacks strong historical precedent. The absence of any score above 0.78 outside of today itself suggests current market conditions represent an outlier state that may have limited historical forward-return guidance. Investors should note that extreme uniqueness in market fingerprints typically correlates with elevated uncertainty in extrapolating historical return patterns.

stock-secrot.dynamic:
# Market Analysis - January 27, 2026

Today's scan identified four strong sector rotation candidates, with SBSW (Sibanye Stillwater) and ESLT (Elbit Systems) leading the pack at 8/9 pattern scores. SBSW stands out dramatically with a 15.4% five-day return and an 83.7% sixty-day gain, paired with an RSI of 77.6 indicating strong momentum, though its position near overbought levels warrants monitoring. The three Industrials picks (ESLT, LHX, RKLB) all show solid 20-60 day performance in the 21-54% range with healthy ADX readings above 40, suggesting confirmed uptrends across the sector. However, pattern_8 (moving average positioning) shows weakness across the board with zero scores, suggesting these stocks may have moved ahead of their technical support levels recently.

hyglqd-credit.dynamic:
# HYG/LQD Credit Spread Analysis - March 13, 2026

Today's HYG/LQD ratio stands at 0.733, placing it at the 45th percentile of its historical range, indicating a neutral positioning between risk-on and risk-off extremes. The ratio has risen 1.27% over the past five days, suggesting a modest recent shift toward risk appetite as high-yield credit (HYG at $79.24) is outperforming investment-grade bonds (LQD at $108.16) relative to their typical relationship. Forward-looking SPY returns show improving win rates as the time horizon extends—5-day win rate is 62.1%, while the 10-day and 20-day win rates climb to 66.0% and 69.8% respectively—with 20-day median returns reaching 1.58%, which is notably positive. This pattern suggests that while the current credit spread is unremarkable, the historical probability of forward SPY gains improves meaningfully when looking beyond one week, particularly at the 20-day horizon.

crash-detection.dynamic: Today's dashboard shows elevated stress signals across multiple dimensions. The lambda_max eigenvalue of 479.36 is exceptionally high, indicating severe correlation clustering among assets—meaning market movements are highly synchronized rather than independent, a hallmark of herding behavior. The magnetization reading of 0.54 reflects strong directional consensus, with the composite score of 70.08 pushing into WARNING territory. The rmt_ratio of 234.55 is critically elevated, suggesting that correlation structure is nearly 235 times stronger than expected in a random market, while 114 out of 1,360 assets show statistically significant eigenvalue contributions. While the Ising model component shows normal risk levels, the extreme concentration in the Random Matrix Theory metrics signals that market participants may be moving in lockstep, potentially limiting diversification benefits and increasing systemic fragility.

advanced-momentum.dynamic: Today's advanced-momentum dashboard shows a market heavily skewed toward caution, with 309 SELL signals and 714 HOLD recommendations dominating 1,383 total assets analyzed. However, 265 STRONG_BUY signals suggest a distinct cohort of assets displaying strong recovery patterns, particularly among the top buy candidates which uniformly show V-shaped trajectories with 90% confidence scores (ABEV, ACMR, AAOI, AEIS, AES, AEP, ALGM, AIG). The extreme scarcity of plain BUY signals (only 23) compared to STRONG_BUY (265) indicates the dashboard is detecting either very compelling recoveries or potential false signals from OBV divergence in a volatile environment. This polarized distribution—with minimal middle-ground positions and heavy concentration at signal extremes—suggests the market may be experiencing sharp directional moves in select securities while most assets remain in consolidation or decline. The consistency of V-shaped patterns across top buys warrants monitoring for sustainability, particularly given the dashboard's sensitivity to price-volume disagreements.

conservative-momentum.dynamic:
# Market Analysis: Conservative-Momentum Dashboard

Today's data shows a tight cluster of moderate momentum candidates with composite scores ranging narrowly from 1.01 to 1.07, suggesting limited standout opportunities in the current market. Only two tickers (SMTOY and SII) pass the safety filter (is_safe=True), with SMTOY showing the strongest recent performance at 6.8% weekly gain and 14.5% monthly gain, though its 12-month return of 269% sits comfortably within the target range. SII presents an interesting contrarian signal with a DIP regime designation and negative weekly performance (-8.1%) despite a solid 26% monthly gain, indicating potential recovery positioning. WDC, CIEN, LASR, and FIX all fail the safety criteria despite reasonable composite scores, with WDC notably extended 6.3% above its 9-day moving average and LASR showing the highest 12-month return at 618% but trailing extension concerns. Overall, the dashboard reflects a cautious environment where most candidates are borderline qualified, making position sizing and entry selectivity particularly important.

macro.dynamic: Today's macro data shows significant market stress signals. The VIX at 27.24 indicates elevated volatility well above the typical 12-18 range, suggesting heightened investor anxiety. SPY is trading below its 50-day moving average (662.25 vs 686.88), and the critical breadth indicator shows zero percent of stocks trading above their 200-day average—an extreme condition that typically precedes major market moves. Treasury spreads remain modestly positive (10y-2y at 0.51), though the high-yield to investment-grade credit ratio of 0.73 signals investors are rotating toward safer bonds. Taken together, these readings point to a risk-off environment with deteriorating market internals despite SPY remaining above its longer-term 200-day support.

regime-changepoint.dynamic: Today's regime-changepoint dashboard signals a significant market transition, with drift reaching an ELEVATED level of 2.27 — indicating substantial deviation from recent market behavior. The dominant regime fingerprint matches a "tariff_shock" pattern at 34.5% similarity, reflecting stagflation concerns likely tied to recent trade policy shifts (such as the referenced Liberation Day tariffs). The second-strongest match is the 2020 COVID crash-recovery at 20.2% similarity, suggesting current market dynamics show some volatility clustering reminiscent of that period, though the tariff regime is materially stronger. AI bull market conditions rank third at only 8.8% similarity, implying the current environment has moved considerably away from the growth-friendly, rate-cut narrative that dominated much of 2024-2025. The elevated drift tier combined with tariff-shock dominance suggests markets are repricing around inflation and policy risk rather than productivity tailwinds.

meta-dashboard.dynamic: Today's data shows a dashboard primarily sourced from four backends (ranker, ranker_long, secrot, and one additional source) across energy and utility sector holdings, with all tickers maintaining HEALTHY verdicts despite limited signal diversity. XLE and VDE lead the short-term rankings (positions 14-15) with the highest ranker scores (84.4 and 84.2), while their long-term equivalents show weaker positioning (67.0 and 72.0 ranks), suggesting near-term relative strength that may not extend to longer timeframes. Utilities (XLU and VPU) rank lower in the aggregate (29-33) and carry bearish spread signals, indicating potential headwinds despite solid health scores in the 89+ range. The dataset appears incomplete, with missing values across advanced_signal, qualifier, and stock_sr_score columns, limiting the cross-dashboard agreement analysis that the meta-dashboard is designed to provide. GSG (commodities) ranks lowest at position 47 with a score of 77.3, rounding out a cautious overall picture where energy outperforms utilities, but full consensus is obscured by data gaps.
